humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers after adding style-guide-version provenance to drafts and narrowing invalidation. Four non-blocking authorization, audit-payload, migration, and regression hardening items remain.

findings.title: Style-guide approval uses broad draft.write authorization
findings.description: Approval authorization is currently tied to draft.write and service-level validation can inspect branch/version state before the repository permission check.

evidence.detail: The style-guide audit identified draft.write as broader than a dedicated style-guide approval permission and noted that branch/latest validation runs before the repository permission gate.

findings.impact: A user allowed to persist drafts may be allowed to approve style policy, and unauthorized callers may learn some style-guide state before denial.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Style-guide approval uses a dedicated permission or a documented policy permission rather than draft.write.
Unauthorized approval attempts are denied before branch/version existence or latest-state details are exposed.
Permission tests cover unauthorized, missing permission, stale version, and successful approval paths.

proposedDagNode.title: Dedicated style-guide approval permission gate
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace broad draft.write authorization for style-guide approval with a dedicated approval permission and fail-closed service ordering.
deliverables: Dedicated style-guide approval permission or documented policy permission
acceptanceCriteria: Approving a style-guide version no longer requires or accepts only draft.write.
Unauthorized callers cannot infer branch or version latest-state details before denial.
Repository and service tests cover missing permission, stale version, and success paths.

findings.title: Primary approval event lacks full approval boundary
findings.description: The primary style-guide version changed event does not carry approver id and the full prior/approved source revision boundary; some boundary details only appear on affected-work invalidation events.

evidence.detail: Initial audit found the acceptance boundary is not fully represented in the primary approval event when no affected references exist.

findings.impact: Audit consumers may miss who approved a policy transition and the exact prior-to-approved boundary if no affected-work invalidation event was emitted.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The primary style-guide approval/change event records approver id, locale branch id, prior version id, approved version id, and source revision boundary.
The event is emitted even when there are no affected draft, QA, export, or benchmark references.
Tests assert the primary event payload independently from affected-work fanout events.

proposedDagNode.title: Style-guide approval audit payload boundary
proposedDagNode.summary: Expand the primary style-guide approval event payload so approval boundaries are audit-complete even when no affected-work fanout exists.
deliverables: Migration or compatibility handling for event consumers if needed
acceptanceCriteria: Primary approval events include approver id, locale branch id, prior version id, approved version id, and source revision boundary.
Approval events remain present when no affected work is found.
Affected-work invalidation events continue to cite the same approval boundary.
auditFocus: Approval audit payload missing actors or boundaries
Divergent primary and fanout event metadata

findings.title: Existing draft rows are not backfilled with style-guide provenance
findings.description: The style_guide_version_id provenance column is nullable and the migration does not backfill existing draft rows with target text, so pre-migration drafts with unknown provenance will not be invalidated by later policy approvals.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit accepted unknown provenance as non-blocking but identified migration behavior risk for existing rows.

findings.impact: Projects created before the provenance migration may retain drafts that should be reconsidered after a later style-guide approval.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A migration or repair command explicitly handles existing draft rows with null style_guide_version_id.
Unknown provenance behavior is documented as either backfilled, invalidated conservatively, or intentionally excluded.
Tests cover first approval after migrating a database with existing drafts.

proposedDagNode.title: Backfill style-guide provenance for existing drafts
proposedDagNode.summary: Handle pre-provenance draft rows so style-guide approval invalidation has explicit behavior for existing non-null target text.
deliverables: Backfill migration or explicit repair command
acceptanceCriteria: Existing draft rows with target text receive deterministic style-guide provenance or explicit unknown-provenance handling.
First later approval after migration does not silently miss drafts that should be reviewed.
Regression tests cover a database migrated from pre-provenance draft rows.
auditFocus: Null style-guide provenance causing missed invalidations
Unsafe assumptions about existing draft rows

findings.title: Draft provenance write path lacks focused DB regression
findings.description: The invalidation regression manually seeds styleGuideVersionId on draft rows, so it does not directly prove the normal saveDrafts or project write path populates provenance.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit observed static code populates style-guide provenance, but requested a focused DB regression to protect that behavior.

findings.impact: A future draft write change could stop recording style-guide provenance while the invalidation test remains green due to manual seed data.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A DB regression writes drafts through the normal project repository path and asserts style_guide_version_id is persisted.
The same test or a companion test proves approval invalidation sees provenance produced by the normal write path.
Manual seed-only coverage is no longer the only proof of draft provenance invalidation.

proposedDagNode.title: Style-guide draft provenance write regression
proposedDagNode.summary: Add DB regression coverage proving normal draft write paths persist style-guide version provenance consumed by invalidation.
deliverables: Approval invalidation test using normally written draft provenance
acceptanceCriteria: Drafts written through the normal repository path persist style_guide_version_id.
Approval invalidation can target drafts using provenance generated by normal writes, not manually seeded rows.
The test fails if saveDrafts stops attaching approved style-guide provenance.
auditFocus: Manual fixture provenance hiding write-path regressions
Draft invalidation tests disconnected from project repository behavior
